title: Meiosperma debile
intro: Meiosperma debile, also known as Debile Meiosperma, is a plant species classified under the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Meiosperma debile are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Meiosperma debile include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are commonly isolated and used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, teas, and extracts, often sold as supplements or used in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Meiosperma debile is classified within the plant family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This family is a part of the order Lamiales, which comprises a diverse group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is further grouped under the clade Asterids, a large clade of flowering plants. Asterids are also part of the larger clade Angiosperm, which includes all flowering plants. The classification of Meiosperma debile reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
